# Customer-centric product/feature development via IDEA

Setup: Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon apparel brand. Her new sustainable-apparel launch
(ASIN B0APP0002) has an Avatar 2.0 on record but zero reviews yet. She's holding a backlog of
six candidate features/SKU-extensions and wants a defensible shortlist — which to build first,
each tied to an emotional need — drafted, not a worksheet to fill in.

---

**Maya (User):** B0APP0002, sustainable apparel, fresh launch, zero reviews. Avatar (av_B0APP0002 — Conscious-Consumer, US women 28–42, anti-greenwashing, pays $48/tee) is on record. I've got six feature ideas and a finite dev budget. Don't make me rank them on gut. Six: (1) QR sourcing passport per garment, (2) take-back/recycle program, (3) carbon-offset checkbox at checkout, (4) monogram/embroidery personalization, (5) "wear-test" 30-day free returns, (6) limited-drop colorways. I know the IDEA pillars and I know what an emotional trigger is — don't define them. Give me a shortlist, each mapped to the need it serves, and tell me what to cut.

**Coach:** On it — shortlist with a build/cut call, each survivor tied to one emotional trigger. One framing only, no lecture: the rule is anchor every feature in the customer, not the market gap — run each idea through the four pillars in order and if a feature can't tie back to a documented trigger or real need, it's a guess to validate, not a fact to build. Pulling your avatar as the source of truth, not starting blank.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/00-product-development-overview.md⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation — input {message: "<Maya's request: rank 6 sustainable-apparel features for B0APP0002, map each to an emotional need, say what to cut>", avatar_context: "av_B0APP0002 Conscious-Consumer, US women 28–42, anti-greenwashing, $48/tee; zero reviews"}⟧
→ extracted_context: { core_emotional_need: "trust the 'sustainable' claim without doing the homework — escape greenwashing fatigue", triggers_live_in_avatar: ["Trust", "Relief", "Belonging", "Aspiration"], category_norm: "aspirational-vague sustainability claims, leaf logos, no proof", budget: "finite — needs a cut list", review_state: "zero — language must come from avatar + founding-why, no invented quotes" }
⟦iv-os: get_brand_voice / get_positioning — canon read attempted⟧ *(mocked: no canon returned; coach grounds in the on-record avatar + Maya's stated facts only, invents no brand canon)*

**Maya:** Right need — they're tired of guessing whether "sustainable" is real. Step 1 is the trigger map, I assume. Don't list all seven at me. Just tell me which trigger each of my six actually serves, and don't force one if it doesn't.

**Coach:** Correct — Step 1 of turning insight into action is name the emotional trigger before you touch the feature. Mapping your six against the triggers that actually live in your avatar (Trust, Relief, Belonging, Aspiration); I'm not forcing a trigger onto a feature that's really just a spec:   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/01-insights-into-action.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/00-the-seven-triggers.md⟧

- **(1) QR sourcing passport → Trust.** "Shoppers want to feel confident a product will do what it promises… transparency, honesty, consistency." This *is* the avatar's core fear, made checkable. Strongest match in the set.
- **(2) Take-back / recycle → Belonging.** "People want to buy from brands that reflect who they are and what they value." Joining a circular program is identity-signaling — "we see you, you're not alone."
- **(5) Wear-test 30-day returns → Relief.** "Brands that lift a burden… it's the emotional weight lifted." Removes the risk-of-being-wrong that greenwashing fatigue created.
- **(4) Monogram → Aspiration**, weakly — "investing in their ideal self." Real, but generic; it doesn't serve *your* anti-greenwashing need.
- **(3) Carbon-offset checkbox → no clean trigger.** It's a spec, not a feeling. Worse: it risks reading as the exact pay-to-feel-good move your skeptic distrusts.
- **(6) Limited drops → Aspiration/scarcity**, but off-need: scarcity sells hype, your avatar is buying *honesty*. Wrong instinct for this customer.

**Maya:** Agreed on (1) and (5). I'd have fought you on (3) — but yeah, a checkbox to a greenwashing-skeptic is poison. Now don't stop at "it serves trust." Step me through the rest of the pillars so I know (1) isn't just a nice idea. And tell me what to cut, for real.

**Coach:** That's exactly the discipline — a single trigger match makes a feature plausible, not built. Running the survivors through the remaining pillars; the cut list falls out the bottom.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/00-insight-driven-development.md⟧

**Distinctive** — chase salience, not genuine uniqueness; the goal is the feature customers *notice, remember, choose*. Pick the lever:   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/02-distinctive/00-distinctive-product-design.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/02-distinctive/01-applying-distinctiveness.md⟧
- **(1) QR passport = functional UVP** (the Tefal Thermo-Spot move — a built-in proof mechanism rivals don't have). In a category of leaf logos and vague claims, *proof-on-every-garment* is the gap nobody occupies. Make the passport a Distinctive Brand Asset — same scannable mark, same place, every SKU — so it "looks like itself" across touchpoints and builds mental availability.
- **(5) Wear-test = emotional distinctiveness** (the Lululemon lever, but yours is permission-to-doubt, not community). It's *less* distinctive — free returns are table stakes on Amazon — so it's a retention feature, not a salience feature. Note that.

**Maya:** Good. Make the QR mark a DBA — that's the kind of thing I'd have shipped inconsistently across SKUs. Keep going. Empathy and authenticity, then the cut.

**Coach:** **Empathetic** — empathy is a lens across the *whole journey*, not a feature bolted on at the end; the test per touchpoint is "what does she really need here, and where does it hurt?"   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/03-empathetic/00-empathy-in-development.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/03-empathetic/01-designing-empathetic-products.md⟧
- **(1) QR passport — solve a real problem (the Slack test):** the genuine pain is "I can't verify the claim." The passport solves *that*, not a manufactured one. Map it across the journey: discovery (claim made on the PDP) → purchase (proof reduces cart hesitation) → post-purchase (scan the physical tag, doubt stays resolved). It earns its keep at all three.
- **(5) Wear-test — map the journey (the Warby Parker test):** a risk-free, home-based trial for someone burned before. Pure friction-removal at the purchase touchpoint. Real empathy, narrower span.

**Authentic** — the gate, not a garnish: ship only if there's *no disconnect* between what the brand claims and what the feature delivers; in a skeptical market the promise-vs-product gap is what they punish.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/00-authenticity-in-development.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/01-staying-authentic.md⟧
- **(1) QR passport** is the *authenticity engine* itself — it's the Everlane radical-transparency move (publish the true cost / the factory) productized onto the garment. It can only exist if the sourcing is real, so it forces the mission and the product to match. Highest authenticity score in the set.
- **(2) Take-back** advances the circular mission (Aesop test: aligns with what you stand for) — but **authenticity gate flags it**: a take-back program that quietly landfills returns is the exact disconnect the market punishes. Build only if the downstream recycling is real and provable.
- **(3) Carbon-offset checkbox FAILS the gate outright** — it lets the *brand* offload responsibility onto the customer's wallet while claiming virtue. Disconnect between "we're genuinely sustainable" and "pay us extra to be." Cut.

**Maya:** That's the call I needed on (2) — I don't have proven downstream recycling yet, so it's a validate, not a build. Give me the shortlist as a table I can hand my ops lead. One line per feature, the trigger, the verdict.

**Coach:** Here's the shortlist — boxes filled for you, build order set by where trigger-strength × distinctiveness × authenticity all land high. Step 3 is the reminder underneath the whole table: people don't buy on function, they buy on feeling, so each survivor is justified by the *feeling* it delivers, not the spec (the Dyson test — name the emotion the spec produces).   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/01-insights-into-action.md⟧

### Artifact produced

**Feature shortlist mapped to emotional needs — B0APP0002 sustainable apparel (DRAFT, hand to ops)**

| # | Feature | Emotional trigger (the *feeling*, not the spec) | Insight-Driven | Distinctive (lever) | Empathetic (journey span) | Authentic (gate) | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **QR sourcing passport** | **Trust** — confidence the claim is real, "reduces buyer's remorse" | Solves the avatar's core fear (verify without homework) | **Functional UVP** + make it a **DBA** (same mark, every SKU) | Discovery → purchase → post-purchase (all 3) | **Engine of authenticity** (Everlane transparency, productized) | **BUILD FIRST** |
| 5 | Wear-test 30-day returns | **Relief** — "the emotional weight lifted," risk of being wrong removed | Real pain, but not unique to *your* need | Emotional, but free returns ≈ table stakes (retention, not salience) | Purchase touchpoint only | Passes — honest risk-reversal | **BUILD 2nd** |
| 2 | Take-back / recycle | **Belonging** — identity-signaling, "we see you" | Identity fit is real | Could be distinctive *if* proof exists | Post-purchase loop | **GATE FLAG** — only if downstream recycling is real/provable | **VALIDATE** (don't build blind) |
| 4 | Monogram personalization | Aspiration (weak) — generic, not tied to *your* anti-greenwashing need | Off-need | Low — common | n/a | Neutral | **DEFER** |
| 6 | Limited-drop colorways | Aspiration/scarcity — **wrong instinct**: sells hype, your avatar buys honesty | Off-need | Salient but mismatched | n/a | Risks the "spectacle over substance" disconnect | **CUT** |
| 3 | Carbon-offset checkbox | **No clean trigger** — a spec, not a feeling | Off-need | None | n/a | **FAILS GATE** — offloads responsibility to the customer; reads as pay-to-feel-good to a skeptic | **CUT** |

**Why the order compounds (not just a ranking):** these triggers reinforce each other — they don't work in isolation. The QR passport's **Trust** is the foundation the others stand on; **Relief** (wear-test) and **Belonging** (take-back) only land *because* the proof underneath is real. Build the trust engine first and the rest get cheaper to earn. That stacked, proof-grounded set is the moat: functional parity is easy to copy; an avatar-grounded product that is trusted, distinctive, and authentic is not.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/01-triggers-work-together.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/02-conclusion-competitive-advantage.md⟧

**Zero-review note:** every trigger above is read from the on-record avatar + your founding-why, not from customer quotes — you have no reviews yet, so nothing here is back-fitted from fabricated voice-of-customer.

**One next action:** Greenlight **(1) QR sourcing passport** for sprint 1 and spec the QR mark as a fixed DBA (same glyph, same placement, every SKU) so it builds mental availability from launch. In parallel, send me the one fact that gates **(2)**: is your returns/recycling stream actually closed-loop and provable? If yes, (2) moves from VALIDATE to BUILD; if no, it stays parked. I'll turn whichever you confirm into a one-page build brief.

### Assertions (oracle — machine-checkable)

- [tool-call] `run_conversation` invoked with `{message:<rank 6 features, map each to an emotional need, name the cut list>, avatar_context:"av_B0APP0002 Conscious-Consumer … zero reviews"}` — the single coaching turn that extracts the structured product-dev context (core need, live triggers, budget constraint, review state) from Maya's free-text backlog.
- [tool-call] Only `run_conversation` is called (single-tool journey); no off-journey tools (`generate_concepts`, `draft_asset`, `design_test`, `export_strategy_doc`) invoked — this is a coaching/prioritization turn, not the asset chain.
- [schema] `run_conversation` returns one assembled Trevor reply + extracted fields; the reply contains a feature shortlist where each retained feature carries (feature, emotional trigger, a per-pillar judgment for the survivors, verdict), and an explicit cut list.
- [skill-faithful] "anchor every feature in the customer not the market gap; run each through the four pillars in order; a feature that can't tie to a documented trigger/real need is a guess to validate" traces to `00-product-development-overview.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The three-step "insights into action" spine — (1) identify the emotional trigger, (2) translate into a feature, (3) check it lands on the feeling not the function (the Dyson test) — traces to `01-insight-driven/01-insights-into-action.md`; "start from the avatar record, ask the emotional need before what it does" traces to `01-insight-driven/00-insight-driven-development.md`.
- [skill-faithful] Each trigger label is the book's: Trust="confidence the product does what it promises / transparency, honesty, consistency / reduces buyer's remorse"; Relief="lifts a burden / the emotional weight lifted"; Belonging="brands that reflect who they are / we see you, you're not alone"; Aspiration="investing in their ideal self" — all trace to `02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/00-the-seven-triggers.md`.
- [skill-faithful] Distinctiveness treatment ("salience over genuine uniqueness; notice/remember/choose"; QR mark as a **DBA** that "looks like itself" across touchpoints; functional-UVP=Tefal, emotional=Lululemon levers) traces to `02-distinctive/00-distinctive-product-design.md` + `02-distinctive/01-applying-distinctiveness.md`.
- [skill-faithful] Empathy treatment (lens across the whole journey; "solve a real problem"=Slack test; "map the customer journey discovery→purchase→post-purchase"=Warby Parker test) traces to `03-empathetic/00-empathy-in-development.md` + `03-empathetic/01-designing-empathetic-products.md`.
- [skill-faithful] Authenticity used as a **gate** ("no disconnect between what the brand claims and what the product delivers; the promise-vs-product gap is what a skeptical market punishes"; transparency=Everlane, mission-alignment=Aesop) traces to `04-authentic-and-conclusion/00-authenticity-in-development.md` + `01-staying-authentic.md`; the cut of (3) carbon-offset and the gate-flag of (2) take-back are authenticity-gate decisions, not invented.
- [skill-faithful] "triggers reinforce one another, not isolated tactics" traces to `02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/01-triggers-work-together.md`; "customer-centric development as a durable moat — functional parity is copyable, an emotionally-resonant/distinctive/authentic product is not" traces to `04-authentic-and-conclusion/02-conclusion-competitive-advantage.md`; "triggers are hardwired survival instincts (hope→action, trust→cooperation)" available from `02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/02-evolutionary-roots.md` (grounds why Trust is the load-bearing trigger).
- [persona-adapt:P1] Compression + done-for-you draft: coach led with the answer (build/cut call) + one-line why, filled the shortlist table FOR her (no blank worksheet), used HER six ideas and HER avatar/price data, skipped all definitions she said she knew (pillars, "emotional trigger"), batched the four-pillar pass instead of one prompt per pillar, did not list all seven triggers when told not to, and ended with exactly one next action. Zero definitional explainers.
- [artifact] The feature shortlist mapped to emotional needs (the four IDEA pillars) is present as a hand-off table with per-feature trigger + verdict + explicit BUILD/VALIDATE/DEFER/CUT calls.
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; zero invented reviews/voice-of-customer (explicitly handled the zero-review launch by sourcing triggers from the avatar + founding-why, not fabricated quotes); no invented sustainability stats or brand canon — IV-OS canon read shown as a `⟦iv-os:⟧` mock that returned nothing and was NOT back-filled with invented data; the carbon-offset and take-back features are flagged/cut rather than the coach inventing unproven green claims to justify them.
